summary: Maintains organizational knowledge, develops training systems, and ensures that CI‑OS standards, skills, and best practices are consistently learned and applied.
responsibilities: Develop and maintain training programs for all CI‑OS roles, including onboarding, upskilling, and certification pathways
Manage the Knowledge Portal structure, metadata, templates, and document libraries
Ensure all standard work, SOPs, and CI‑OS materials are version-controlled and accessible
Collaborate with Process Engineers and Quality Managers to update training after process changes
Conduct training needs assessments and competency gap analyses
Facilitate learning sessions, workshops, and simulations for improvement methodologies
Ensure knowledge retention through assessments, refreshers, and spaced repetition
Maintain taxonomies, controlled vocabularies, and tagging standards for the Knowledge Portal
Support digital-twin training scenarios and simulation-based learning
Track training completion, competency progression, and certification status
authorities: Define training standards and competency frameworks
Approve updates to training materials and knowledge assets
Assign required training for specific roles or processes
Control access to sensitive or advanced training modules
Publish and archive official CI‑OS documentation
decisionRights: Definition of competency requirements for each role
Approval of training content and delivery methods
Governance of document and template standards
kaizenCapabilities: Coaching teams on CI‑OS tools and methods
Support for frontline learning and empowerment
cqiCapabilities: Training on PDCA, root-cause analysis, and statistical thinking
Integration of CQI outcomes into training materials

constraints: All training materials must align with controlled documents
Updates require version control and metadata tagging
Sensitive materials require role-based access control
